evals.prompt: Set up my MatrixScan Count app to scan against a fixed list of expected barcodes with these quantities: 7610200010148 ×3, 7886459920525 ×1, 7617400031003 ×5, 7613312015513 ×2. File: IntegratedBarcodeCountViewController.swift
evals.expected_output: The skill builds a Set<TargetBarcode> with TargetBarcode(data:quantity:) for the four codes and their quantities, creates a BarcodeCountCaptureList(listener:targetBarcodes:), applies it with barcodeCount.setCaptureList(...), and makes the view controller conform to BarcodeCountCaptureListListener implementing captureList(_:didUpdate:) and captureList(_:didCompleteWith:). The existing integration (context, camera, settings, mode, view, listener, lifecycle) is otherwise unchanged.

assertions.text: A Set<TargetBarcode> is built using TargetBarcode(data:quantity:) for all four codes with the correct quantities: 7610200010148 quantity 3, 7886459920525 quantity 1, 7617400031003 quantity 5, 7613312015513 quantity 2
A BarcodeCountCaptureList is created via BarcodeCountCaptureList(listener:targetBarcodes:) with the target-barcode set and a listener
barcodeCount.setCaptureList(captureList) is called to apply the list

The view controller conforms to BarcodeCountCaptureListListener and implements captureList(_:didUpdate:) and captureList(_:didCompleteWith:) — the Swift selectors are didUpdate / didCompleteWith, NOT didUpdateSession / didCompleteWithSession
The session parameter type is BarcodeCountCaptureListSession (and if progress is read, it uses real properties like correctBarcodes / missingBarcodes — not invented ones)
The existing integration is preserved: context, camera setup, BarcodeCountSettings/symbologies, BarcodeCount mode, BarcodeCountView, BarcodeCountListener, uiDelegate and lifecycle are unchanged
No not-in-list accept/reject action or other extra features are added unless needed (basic list scanning only)

evals.prompt: My MatrixScan Count app already scans against a fixed list. When a scanned barcode isn't on the list, let the user accept or reject it, and change the buttons so accept says "Keep" and reject says "Discard". File: IntegratedBarcodeCountListScanningViewController.swift
evals.expected_output: Starting from an integration that already has a capture list, the skill enables the not-in-list action by creating a BarcodeCountNotInListActionSettings, setting enabled = true and acceptButtonText = "Keep" / rejectButtonText = "Discard", and assigning it to barcodeCountView.barcodeNotInListActionSettings. The existing capture-list setup and the rest of the integration are unchanged.

assertions.text: A BarcodeCountNotInListActionSettings is created (BarcodeCountNotInListActionSettings()) and assigned to barcodeCountView.barcodeNotInListActionSettings
Its enabled property is set to true (the default is false)
acceptButtonText is set to "Keep" and rejectButtonText is set to "Discard"
It uses the real property names on BarcodeCountNotInListActionSettings (enabled / acceptButtonText / rejectButtonText) — no guessed/invented API

The existing capture list (BarcodeCountCaptureList / setCaptureList / BarcodeCountCaptureListListener) and the rest of the integration (context, camera, settings, mode, view, listener, uiDelegate, lifecycle) are left unchanged; no unrelated features added

evals.prompt: My MatrixScan Count app scans against a list. Change the highlight colors: not-in-list barcodes orange, accepted barcodes blue, rejected barcodes cyan. File: IntegratedBarcodeCountListScanningViewController.swift
evals.expected_output: Starting from an integration that already has a capture list, the skill sets the per-state brushes — barcodeCountView.notInListBrush to orange, acceptedBrush to blue, rejectedBrush to cyan, each via Brush(fill:stroke:strokeWidth:). Because brushes only render in the Dot style, the BarcodeCountView is created with style: .dot (or the response notes it). The existing capture list and the rest of the integration are unchanged.

assertions.text: barcodeCountView.notInListBrush is set to an orange Brush, acceptedBrush to a blue Brush, and rejectedBrush to a cyan Brush (or the equivalent brushForRecognizedBarcodeNotInList / brushForAcceptedBarcode / brushForRejectedBarcode delegate callbacks)
Brushes are constructed with Brush(fill:stroke:strokeWidth:) — not fillColor:/strokeColor:
It uses the correct state names (notInListBrush / acceptedBrush / rejectedBrush) — NOT the non-existent brushForUnrecognizedBarcode, and no icon API
Because brushes only render in the Dot style, the BarcodeCountView is created with style: .dot OR the response notes that brush colors apply to the Dot style
The existing capture list (target barcodes + setCaptureList + BarcodeCountCaptureListListener) and the rest of the integration are left unchanged
